overview: Naya Ephemerate is a tempo strategy that leverages cheap creatures and efficient board-clearing spells to control the pace of the game while applying steady pressure. The deck uses pump effects and graveyard recursion to maintain resource advantage and swing combat trades in its favor. Its multicolor identity provides flexible answers and resilient game plans tailored to the Pauper environment.
win_conditions: Overwhelming the opponent with pumped creatures and combat damage
Closing out games with direct damage from board-clearing spells
Outlasting opponents through graveyard recursion and card advantage

keep_criteria: Hands with one or two cheap creatures to establish early presence
Hands containing Malevolent Rumble paired with a threat or recursion
Hands with Writhing Chrysalis for early graveyard interaction
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (prioritize duals or shocklands for fixing) and at least one Ephemerate or bounce enabler.
Keep hands with 1-2 mana creatures that have ETB/DTB triggers or can be bounced for value.
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking early plays or interaction
Hands with only high-cost cards or multiple lands
Hands missing color fixers for key spells
Mulligan to pure land hands, hands with only 3+ mana creatures, or hands lacking bounce/removal in fast formats.

early_game: Deploy low-cost creatures to establish board presence and force trades
Use Sacred Cat to immediately boost early threats and enable favorable exchanges
Hold up interaction or recursion to disrupt opponent's opening sequence
Turn 1: Play a land, optionally cast a 1-mana creature or play a shockland if fixing is critical.
Turn 2: Cast a 2-mana creature, then use Ephemerate to bounce it back to hand to trigger its ETB effect, or bounce an opponent's threat if interaction is needed.
If drawing Writhing Chrysalis, play it on turn 2 or 3, then bounce it with Ephemerate to trigger its death effect and gain flying/tempo.
mid_game: Cast Malevolent Rumble to clear opposing boards while dealing direct damage
Leverage Writhing Chrysalis deaths to recover key spells or creatures from the graveyard
Maintain tempo by playing creatures on opponent's turn when possible to disrupt their rhythm
Preserve Ephemerate for high-value targets (your own ETB triggers or opponent's key threats).
Don't overcommit mana to bounce loops if you can't follow up.
Use shocklands strategically to maintain mana flexibility.
Track graveyard resources if Writhing Chrysalis or other DTB effects are involved.
Commit threats when you have interaction ready to protect them or bounce opponent's blockers.
Hold back Ephemerate if the opponent is likely to have removal or board wipes.
late_game: Stabilize with recycled threats and pumped survivors to apply consistent pressure
Force opponent into unfavorable trades using board clears and combat math
Use remaining resources to close out the game or outvalue control strategies
Deploy a high-impact creature with a strong ETB/DTB effect, then use Ephemerate to bounce it for a second trigger, or bounce it to hand to cast it again.
If Writhing Chrysalis is in play, bounce it to trigger flying/+1/+1, then attack with a buffed threat.
Use Ephemerate on opponent's blockers to clear the path.
Go all-in when you have multiple bounce enablers, a clear path to damage, and the opponent has no interaction left.
Commit when you can trigger multiple ETB/DTB effects in a single turn to close the game.

key_cards.name: Sacred Cat
key_cards.notes: Cast on curve to immediately boost a threat, enabling favorable trades and early pressure. Use it to push lethal or survive aggressive openings.

key_cards.name: Malevolent Rumble
key_cards.notes: Use to clear opposing boards while chipping away at the opponent's life total. Time it to maximize board swing and direct damage impact.

key_cards.name: Writhing Chrysalis
key_cards.notes: Sacrifice or trade early to retrieve key spells or creatures from the graveyard. Maintain card advantage and keep your game plan flexible.

sequencing_tips: Hold up colors for Malevolent Rumble before committing to a full board push
Trigger Writhing Chrysalis carefully to ensure you can recover high-impact cards
Time Sacred Cat to maximize combat damage or enable a lethal strike
Prioritize playing creatures on opponent's turn to disrupt their sequencing
Ephemerate only targets creatures, so plan your bounce loops carefully.
Writhing Chrysalis's flying trigger is powerful but temporary
use it to bypass ground blockers or finish the game.
Avoid bouncing your own creatures if you don't have a follow-up trigger.
Watch out for board wipes that can reset your bounce loops.
Practice stack sequencing to maximize ETB/DTB triggers without giving the opponent a chance to respond.

matchups.advice: Prioritize early blockers and use Malevolent Rumble to reset the board while dealing damage.
Apply steady pressure and use recursion to outlast their resource base.
Race them with pumped threats and use tempo spells to disrupt their curve.
Disrupt their setup with early interaction and use board clears to delay their win.
Race them before they deploy large threats and use recursion to maintain pressure.

Use Ephemerate on your own creatures to trigger ETB effects before they're countered, or bounce opponent's counterspellers if possible. Cast spells in response to their interaction, or use cheap, uncounterable threats if available. Rely on instant-speed bounce to bypass stack interaction.

Win the resource war by drawing more cards and having better bounce sequencing. Prioritize keeping Ephemerate for high-value triggers. Use targeted removal to eliminate their threats before they can bounce them. Control the board state by trading efficiently and forcing them to use interaction on low-value targets.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells, board wipes, and targeted removal.
Note if the opponent is saving interaction for your bounce targets.
Track graveyard accumulation if they play discard or mill.
Pay attention to mana development—if they're playing extra lands or ramp spells, they're likely ahead on tempo.
